title: #[non_exhaustive] on AppEvent and AppCommand is justified by protocol extensibility
description: Both AppEvent and AppCommand carry #[non_exhaustive]. These enums mirror protocol-defined sets: AppEvent variants correspond to JMAP server events (session ready, chat changes, message updates, SSE state changes), and AppCommand variants correspond to user-initiated JMAP operations (select chat, send message, mark read). The JMAP Chat spec is not frozen; future revisions will add new event types (e.g. typing indicators, presence, read receipts as first-class events) and new command types. Without #[non_exhaustive], adding a variant is a breaking change for any crate that matches on these enums. With it, downstream match arms that lack a wildcard arm produce a compile error, forcing explicit handling rather than silent fallthrough. A future contributor might remove #[non_exhaustive] arguing that these are internal types — they are currently internal, but the attribute costs nothing and the spec extensibility rationale holds regardless of visibility.
close_reason: Good decision already implemented in current codebase
